description:
Quantification and analysis of 3' tagged-end single-cell sequencing data using Salmon's Alevin (v1.5.2) function.
See\: The Alevin Documentation for detailed usage guidelines.
author: Anthony S. Castanza;UCSD-MesirovLab
plan:
## Salmon.Alevin.Quant GenePattern Module — Implementation Plan ### 1. Overview Salmon.Alevin.Quant is a GenePattern module that Quantification and analysis of 3' tagged-end single-cell sequencing data using Salmon's Alevin (v1.5.2) function.
\n\nSee\: The Alevin Documentation\nfor detailed usage guidelines. ### 2. Parameters The module exposes 18 parameters: - Required: Barcodes, Reads, Transcriptome.Index, Chemistry, Library.Type, TgMap, Output.Basename, GTF.Gene.ID.Type, mtRNA.From.GTF, rRNA.From.GTF, dumpMTX, expectCells, forceCells, dumpFeatures, numCellBootstraps - Optional: Whitelist, mtRNA, rRNA ### 3. Command Line ``` salmon_alevin_quant.sh -b -q -i -c -l -t -z -u -w -m -n -r -s -x -e -f -d -a -j ``` ### 4. Docker Image Docker image: genepattern/salmon-alevin-quant\:beta ### 5. Validation Strategy - Validate all required parameters are provided - Validate file formats match expected extensions - Check parameter value ranges where applicable ### 6. Implementation Roadmap Phase 1: Wrapper script implementation Phase 2: Docker container setup Phase 3: Parameter validation and error handling Phase 4: Testing and documentation

parameters.name: Barcodes
parameters.description: Cellular barcodes+UMI file(s), the FASTQ file containing CB+UMI raw sequences. Alevin also supports parsing of data from multiple files as long as the order is the same as in the "Reads" parameter.

parameters.name: Reads
parameters.description: The FASTQ file containing raw read-sequences. Alevin also supports parsing of data from multiple files as long as the order is the same as in the "Barcodes" parameter.

parameters.name: Transcriptome.Index
parameters.description:
An Indexed transcriptome created with `Salmon Indexer`.
\nMust be in .tar.gz format.

parameters.name: Chemistry
parameters.description:
The chemistry used by the single-cell sequencing platform.
\nDrop-Seq, 10x Chromium v2, 10x Chromium v3, CITE-Seq, CEL-Seq, CEL-Seq2, and Quartz-Seq2 are currently supported. BD Rhapsody support is experimental.

parameters.name: Library.Type
parameters.description:
The library type of the RNA-seq. This describes the relative orientation of paired end reads. \n
\nThe Alevin authors recommend using ISR (default) for both Drop-seq and Chromium chemistry.

choices.display: ISF (Inward, read 1 from the forward strand)
ISR (Inward, read 1 from the reverse strand)
OSF (Outward, read 1 from the forward strand)
OSR (Outward, read 1 from the reverse strand)

parameters.name: TgMap
parameters.description:
A two column tsv (tab-separated) file with no header containing the transcript to gene map file. The first column lists each transcript present in the reference, the second column lists the corresponding gene.
Alternatively, a .GTF file can be supplied to automatically create a transcript to gene map.

parameters.name: Output.Basename
parameters.description: Base name to use for outputting the alevin quantification results. Should be made unique for downstream processing.

parameters.name: GTF.Gene.ID.Type
parameters.description:
If supplying a GTF file to automatically construct the transcript to gene map (TgMap parameter), map the transcripts to\:
the Gene ID (GTF.Gene.ID.Type \= gene_id) eg\: ENSG00000141510
the Gene Symbol (GTF.Gene.ID.Type \= gene_symbol) eg\: TP53
This requires a GTF file to be supplied to "TgMap"

parameters.name: Whitelist
parameters.description:
Optionally explicitly specify the whitelist cellular barcodes to use for cell detection and cellular barcode sequence correction. If not given, alevin generates its own set of putative cellular barcodes.
Note\: Not 10x 724k whitelist
This flag does not use the biologically known whitelist provided by 10x, instead it's the per experiment level whitelist file e.g. the file generated by cellranger with the name barcodes.tsv.

parameters.name: mtRNA
parameters.description:
The list of mitochondrial genes which are to be used as a feature for cellular barcode whitelising naive Bayes classification.
Note\: It is generally advisable to not use nuclear mitrochondrial genes in this as they can be both up and/or down regulated which might cancel out the usefulness of this feature. Please check issue \#367 in salmon repo to know more about it.

parameters.name: mtRNA.From.GTF
parameters.description:
If supplying a GTF file to automatically construct the transcript to gene map (TgMap parameter), automatically construct the mtRNA list from the GTF directly.
Note\: if set to true, this will override the list supplied to the "mtRNA" parameter.
This requires a GTF file to be supplied to "TgMap"

parameters.name: rRNA
parameters.description: The list of ribosomal genes which are to be used as a feature for cellular barcode whitelising naive Bayes classification.\n

parameters.name: rRNA.From.GTF
parameters.description:
If supplying a GTF file to automatically construct the transcript to gene map (TgMap parameter), automatically construct the rRNA list from the GTF directly.
Note\: if set to true, this will override the list supplied to the "rRNA" parameter.
This requires a GTF file to be supplied to "TgMap"

parameters.name: dumpMTX
parameters.description: Convert the default binary format of alevin for gene-count matrix into a human readable mtx (matrix market exchange) sparse format. Generates a new output called quants_mat.mtx in the compressed output folder.

parameters.name: expectCells
parameters.description:
This command line flag uses the cellranger type white-listing procedure. As specified in their algorithm overview page, "All barcodes whose total UMI counts exceed m/10 are called as cells", where m is the frequency of the top 1% cells as specified by the parameter of this command line flag.\n
\nNote\: Setting this parameter to "0" disables this option and, unless forceCells is specified, instead uses the default Alevin knee calculation heuristics.

parameters.name: forceCells
parameters.description:
By default, Alevin performs a heuristic based initial cellular barcode white-listing by finding the knee in the distribution of the barcode frequency. With this flag, by looking at the barcode frequency distribution, a user can explicitly specify the number of CB to consider for initial white-listing.\n
\nNote\: Setting this parameter to "0" disables this option and, unless expectCells is specified, instead uses the default Alevin knee calculation heuristics.

parameters.name: dumpFeatures
parameters.description: If activated, alevin dumps all the features used by the CB classification and their counts at each cell level. Generally used in pair with other flags.

parameters.name: numCellBootstraps
parameters.description:
Estimate the inferential uncertainty in the estimation of per cell level gene count matrix by performing bootstrapping of the reads in per-cell level equivalence classes, and generate the mean and variance of the count matrix. This option generates three additional files, namely, quants_mean_mat.gz, quants_var_mat.gz and quants_boot_rows.txt. The format of the files stay the same as quants_mat.gz while the row order is saved in quants_boot_rows.txt and the column order is stays the same as in file quants_mat_cols.txt.
\nSet the number of bootstrap estimates to perform (0 \= flag will not be passed to alevin). dumpFeatures must be set to "true".
